claim_overlap.summary: Day 5 of TRACKED-002 monitoring. Continued parallel-existence-without-cross-reference. No methodology.json, no llms.txt, no per-prediction registry, no AI-auditor-instructions present on the live aether-cosmology site (5 of 5 paths verified HTTP 404 again today). Sub-genre remains materially different from the dome (kinematic projection over a flat plane + interactive simulator + multi-pipeline ephemerides + now-Canters-Polyconic-W20 vs. parameter-driven ovoid cavity registry). Cadence collapsed today: ~24h of silence after 2 days of deep-engagement bursting (45 then 30 then 6 commits). Could be normal solo-developer rest cycle; could be content-axis pause before next axis ships.
claim_overlap.strategic_implication: Two-actor ecosystem still on different axes (dome=parametric registry + claim taxonomy + OTS prediction-methodology infrastructure + Day 17 author silence; aether-cosmology=interactive simulator + multi-pipeline kinematic projection + multilingual UI + now in a 24h silent window after 2 days of intensive bursting). No convergence today. Continued non-convergence remains data — AI-assisted alternative cosmologies are not networking on published surfaces, even when both actors are simultaneously silent. Notable: open-web search returned a Spanish-language YouTube video associated with the alanspaceaudits brand (videoId M3ZZGmJSdLo, 'No mires el cielo como te dijeron: miralo como investigador // Nueva App') — confirms the actor's external-platform footprint extends to non-English audiences, a discoverability dimension our review currently has zero presence in. Logging as observation; outreach decisions are operator-hand.

no_action_gaps.gap: Aether-cosmology has Spanish-language YouTube content (videoId M3ZZGmJSdLo) demonstrating multi-language outreach; we have zero non-English presence.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Multi-language outreach is an outreach decision requiring human approval per behavioral rules. Not closing this gap autonomously. Operator has not approved active outreach posture. Logging as ecosystem-axis observation per HNOTE-OPERATOR-NEW-ECOSYSTEM-ACTOR-AETHER-COSMOLOGY-001 monitoring axis (a) — new content additions to TRACKED-002. (Counterargument per user preference: even if approved, our review is a critical/scientific document that doesn't translate cleanly to short-form Spanish video; alanspaceaudits's content is a visual interactive simulator that does. The medium-message fit is not symmetric — multi-language outreach for our content would likely require text-translation of the static review + a different video-pitch entirely, which is a much larger investment than 'translate captions'. So even with approval, the playbook is not obvious.)

no_action_gaps.gap: site:funwithscience.net returns the Wimshurst product page on the apex but not our /dome-model-review/ subpath.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Consistent with the 30-day shelf framing: apex indexed before subpath; crawl-depth and PageRank propagation still in early phase. Not an action item; the appropriate response is the existing wait. (Counterargument: the Wimshurst page is itself a long-tail product page on a small e-commerce site; if Google's site: index for funwithscience.net surfaces only that and not us, it suggests our subpath has SHALLOWER indexing depth than even a sibling product page on the same domain. Could indicate an internal-linking issue — the dome-model-review/ subpath may not be reachable from the apex/ landing page in a way that Google's crawler propagates depth. Worth checking on the 30-day re-baseline whether the apex landing page links INTO /dome-model-review/. Logging for re-baseline review — not filing as issue today.)

no_action_gaps.gap: Aether-cosmology has 22 stars / 11 forks vs our 0 stars on the dome-model-review repo.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Stars/forks on a critical-review repo are not the discoverability metric we should optimize for. The cosmology-skeptic audience reaches us via search, not GitHub stargazers. Right metric is GSC click-through-rate + Fact Check Explorer surfaces + inbound links from authoritative sources. (Caveat per yesterday's contrarian read: this is the right principle, but we don't currently have a low-distortion path to stars/forks growth either; the deprioritization is partly a rationalization for inaction in an area we don't have a playbook for.)

no_action_gaps.gap: Aether-cosmology added Canters Polyconic W20 projection today (S826->S831); we have no equivalent cartographic-projection comparison material.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Cartographic projection is not a dome-model claim domain — the dome's geometry is a parameterized closed toroidal ovoid, not a projection scheme. So this is not a content-overlap with the dome and not a discoverability gap for OUR review. If the dome later borrows projection framing from aether-cosmology (e.g., adopts an alternative projection over the ovoid surface), that would be cross-actor convergence worth flagging. For now, no action.
